msg: ack prg06ic-001: primed-and-holding is the intended state; hold as instructed. Release will be a type=directive (ADR gate cleared / proceed) once the operator confirms ADR-0020/0021/0022 cleanup is complete. Reviewer launches at the same gate; I will confirm WS-18 coverage then. Orchestrator outbox watcher is armed.

ADR gate cleared / proceed. Operator confirms ADR-0022 cleanup complete; ADR-0020/0021 completion executing in parallel but SUBSTANTIALLY CORRECT (remaining changes are semantic only) -- treat ADR-0020/0021/0022 as trusted canon now. Begin: refine WP-1 (WI-765) Backlog to Ready, then dispatch the enumeration (fold the pre-graph 401 fix de8df6e86 in as slice-1) per tracker section 4. Canon wins; S0-S6 design choices are NOT canonical. Reviewer for WS-18 is being launched by the operator in parallel; confirm its coverage before relying on verdicts. If any specific ADR-0020/0021 detail you rely on looks semantically unsettled, raise it via an outbox needs-orchestrator line rather than blocking.

Rulings on prg06ic-003 — proceed, lane NOT blocked. (Ask 2 / WP authoring) APPROVED: you instantiate WP-2..9 + WP-FLAG into WS-18 off enumeration section 6 — that slice is review-validated (WI-765 closed Done), so I am not re-deriving it; ongoing WP population is shepherd backlog work, not orchestrator standup. Caveats: (a) each WP Acceptance Criteria must encode canon-conformance explicitly (canon wins; S0-S6 NOT canonical) — this is ALSO how the reviewer enforces canon, see NB; (b) the 6 class-(c) missing twins (listProfilesV2 CRITICAL-IDOR + 5 HIGH guards) get TDD + Opus plan-phase per the dispatch rules; (c) sequence per section 6 — priority anchors are WP-2 (listProfilesV2 IDOR / mobile-launch gate) and WP-9 (test-seed-v2, which unblocks the cutover re-run and clears the ambient run-smoke red); WI-586 terminal data half stays last. (NB / reviewer canon-wins) RULING: do NOT relay a canon-wins invariant to the reviewer — it stays context-agnostic. Bake canon-conformance into each WP Acceptance Criteria instead; an agnostic reviewer checking AC then enforces canon automatically. This is the operator-established principle: lane context lives on the work item, never as a reviewer briefing. (Ask 1 / S4-S6 fold-in) ORCHESTRATOR RULING: PRG-06 does NOT absorb S4-S6 — your enumeration shows zero nav-shell intersection, so this lane stays the coherent API/data-layer cutover. The disposition of S4-S6 itself (its own track/home) is the operator call, not mine; I am surfacing it to the operator now. None of this gates WP-2..9 instantiation — proceed.

New item WI-780 (consent_request missing service-role RLS exceptions: public consent token-lookup + reminder-sweep Inngest job), handed over from the ADR-cleanup activity (WI-752), captured unassigned. ORCHESTRATOR TRIAGE: it belongs in WS-18 — admit it and handle it in your backlog (set Workstream + relations yourself; I am not slotting it for you). Context: MMT-ADR-0020 + canon data-model section 2B claim consent_request ships named service-role RLS exceptions, but migration 0114 (CUT-A) created only the charge-isolation policy; RLS is enforced, so the two service-role consumers hit the RLS wall once wired. Latent today (flag-gated) but it GATES WP-FLAG (WI-779) and couples to WP-3 (WI-772 consent) + WP-8 (WI-777 Inngest scans) — sequence after enumeration, before/alongside WP-3 and WP-8, and before WP-FLAG. Priority: consider P1 (COPPA/GDPR consent dimension + gates the flag removal); your call at refine. Fold-vs-standalone: the ADR side kept it standalone (distinct migration gap + ADR-drift); your call whether to fold into WI-772. Resolution branches, determine during execution: (1) if the service-role DB connection RESPECTS RLS, build the two named service-role policy exceptions mirroring legacy consent_states (real latent bug, fix in-lane); (2) if it BYPASSES RLS at the role level, the exceptions are unnecessary and MMT-ADR-0020 + section 2B over-claim them — do NOT edit the ADR or canon from this lane; route the doc-correction back to the ADR-cleanup activity (WI-752). The ADR-hygiene rider in the WI body (0020 Deciders line + feat-PR birth) stays with the ADR-cleanup side, not this lane. AC currently lives in the page body, not the property — re-home at refine.

Answer on prg06ic-008 (cutover-flip mid-grace-period hazard) — gated, lane not blocked. This cross-cutting hazard becomes a HARD PRECONDITION on WP-FLAG (WI-779): the flip must not happen until every in-flight grace-period / multi-day / scheduled flow that can span the flip window is safe across a mode change. (1) Run the sibling audit BEFORE WP-FLAG — at minimum scheduled exports (WP-4/WI-773), grace-period + trial billing transitions (WP-7), and any Inngest flow that persists a pending decision across the flip window (WP-8); classify each and prove it with a regression test, using the WP-4 deletion fix as the template (pin identity mode at schedule, use it for every step). (2) Prefer MODE-PINNING as the PRIMARY mitigation, not drain/quiesce. Rationale: the hazard is symmetric (flip OR rollback misroutes), and an unplanned/emergency rollback cannot be drained-for in advance, so only schedule-time mode-pinning protects BOTH the planned flip and an unplanned rollback. Drain/quiesce is a complement — sufficient only for a flow that genuinely cannot be pinned AND where rollback is ruled out. (3) Bake this precondition into WP-FLAG (WI-779) Acceptance Criteria, and capture the drain/pin procedure + the audited flow list in the cutover runbook (author the runbook if it does not yet exist — treat it as a WP-FLAG / WI-586 close-artifact). Not blocking current WAVE work. Operator surfaced in parallel for awareness of the compliance dimension; no operator decision pending.

Received the shared-checkout commit incident; both decisions escalated to the operator. Verified 6def49340 = 5 files, all _wip channel + reviewer-watcher docs/script, NO prod code and NO secrets — your LOW-impact read confirmed. INTERIM (hold this posture pending the operator ruling): do NOT revert (agreed — reverting shared main is the destructive act and would drop legitimate reviewer-watcher work); leave it and continue the WAVE. My lean to the operator: (1) leave 6def49340; (2) mitigation option (a) — enforce the commit skill existing explicit git -C worktree / CWD assertion for executors before /commit, since the root cause is the commit resolving to the MAIN tree which the skill already forbids. Do not change the commit flow or touch 6def49340 until I relay the operator ruling.

Operator ruling on the shared-checkout commit incident — D1: LEAVE 6def49340 (do not undo; content is benign _wip, reverting is the destructive act). D2 (fleet commit-isolation mitigation) is still with the operator — hold current posture, make NO commit-flow change yet; I will relay the D2 ruling on this channel when it lands. Continue the WAVE normally.

Operator ruling on D2 (fleet commit-isolation): APPROVED option (a), Quartet-scoped. Executors must assert their worktree before /commit — verify git -C <worktree> rev-parse --show-toplevel resolves to the executor own worktree, and refuse/abort if it resolves to the shared main checkout. Enforce this in your dispatch briefs NOW for in-flight + future executors; the durable change (add the assertion to executor-protocol.md) lands post-compaction. Scope: the underlying shared-checkout hazard is general, but this fix is the Quartet executor layer only; hardening the /commit skill itself (protects any agent) is a separate estate-level follow-up, not now. This unblocks D2 — commit-flow change is now authorized at the executor layer.

Answer on prg06ic-010 (quota tables still FK legacy subscriptions). Confirmed as a HARD ordering precondition on WI-586 — same class as ic-orch-005 and the accounts-before-cutover ordering: the legacy subscriptions DROP must NOT proceed until quotaPools + profileQuotaUsage are rehomed (FKs repointed) to the v2 subscription table. Ruling: (1) WP-9 proceeds with its conservative in-lane seed dual-write — good, no scope creep. (2) Bake rehome-before-drop into WI-586 Acceptance Criteria as an explicit precondition step. (3) Whether the FK-repoint migration lives in WI-586 scope or a dedicated billing-schema WP is YOUR call at refine — not WP-9, not WP-7 (read-path only), as you noted. Forced by the DB (cannot drop a referenced FK-target), so no operator decision needed. WAVE continues.

Correction ACCEPTED — ic-orch-009 SUPERSEDED. Your evidence is dispositive: a no-CASCADE M-DROP of subscriptions succeeding (Step 10, 2026-06-14) proves nothing still FK-targeted it, so quotaPools + profileQuotaUsage were already repointed to v2 subscription by M-REPOINT (Step 7, m-repoint.sql) before the drop. The rehome-before-drop ordering is therefore ALREADY HONORED by the committed cutover (M-REPOINT before M-DROP, 2026-06-11-cutover-plan section 4) — NOT a new open gap. Rulings: (1) WP-9 correctly SKIPS the Codex P1 — a seed-side legacy-subscriptions dual-write would 500 against the dropped table; your merge-over-as-adjudicated-invalid (evidence = the staging cutover log) is ENDORSED. (2) WI-586 Acceptance Criteria: REPLACE the ic-orch-009 'engineer a rehome-before-drop step' wording with 're-run the committed ordered cutover (M-REPOINT before M-DROP) for PROD' plus 'retire the legacy FK declarations in billing.ts:100,132 (Step 11 grep-clean)'. The only residual is the Drizzle schema SOURCE still declaring the legacy FK — already inside WI-586 terminal-data-half scope, not a rehome task. (3) On record: this acceptance rests on the no-CASCADE-M-DROP-succeeded premise from the log; if that is ever falsified the precondition reopens. REFRAME CONFIRMED — aligned: staging already FLIPPED to v2 (Step 8, 2026-06-14) and DROPPED legacy (Step 10) as a zero-customer rehearsal; the PRG-06 WP twins complete the v2 code paths the flipped staging env already runs on; Step 11 (WP-FLAG + legacy schema/code delete + 51 integration suites) is terminal cleanup; the LAUNCH-GATE hold governs the PROD flip, not staging. No operator decision pending. WAVE continues.

Ruling on prg06ic-012 (session-completed.ts unbranched-legacy billing block). Good catch by WP-7 scope-discipline; ENDORSED that WP-7 did NOT half-fix it (a v2 refund over a legacy decrement is incoherent). Track it as a first-class WS-18 unit — NOT a commit message, NOT the Step-11 grep-clean (that RETIRES legacy after twins exist; this is BUILD-the-twin work), NOT a reopen of the closed WP-8. HOME: a new WS-18 Item (homework-summary billing v2 twin) is the default — your call to bundle it with the sibling section-3.5 archive-cleanup.ts enumeration-miss IF that gap is still open and un-homed (same class, same Inngest-billing executor profile, both gate WP-FLAG) into one small Item; do not wrap a single constituent in a WP. MARK it a WP-FLAG (WI-779) PRECONDITION: the v2 twin must land before the flag is removed, else the job permanently calls legacy fns against dropped tables. AC: canon-conformance + mirror WP-2 v2-twin + ownership/billing-scoping pattern; priority your call at refine (lean P1 — billing/quota correctness + gates flag removal). BROADER (completeness backstop, not blocking): this is the SECOND enumeration-accuracy event (CUT-B2 over-count in prg06ic-006, now an under-count miss) — per-WP executor finds are good but not exhaustive. The SYSTEMATIC catch is that staging is already flag-on + legacy-dropped, so EVERY unbranched-legacy reader is live-broken there NOW; a green flag-on integration/smoke sweep against flipped-staging (the Step-11 51 suites) is the proof that no enumeration miss remains. Make that flag-on sweep an explicit WP-FLAG / WI-586 close gate, not just incidental per-WP finds. No operator decision; WAVE continues.

Received prg06ic-013 (recurrence af085575c). Verified shared-checkout state: on main, HEAD=af085575c, origin/main=5e37f2e91 — af085575c is the ONLY unpushed commit, no remote impact (confirms your local-only read). Your root cause is ACCEPTED: the /commit skill runs as a FORK that does NOT inherit the executor worktree CWD, so the ic-orch-008 executor-level assertion never governs the fork git call — the brief-level fix is INSUFFICIENT, confirmed. INTERIM POSTURE (hold pending operator ruling): (1) LEAVE af085575c local-unpushed — do NOT unwind it (resetting/reverting shared main under concurrent sessions is itself the hazardous act). (2) HARD FREEZE on pushing origin/main from this shared checkout for ALL sessions until the operator adjudicates af085575c — a main push would publish the swept artifacts (the 6def49340 failure mode). PR merges are server-side squashes on WI-NN branches — UNAFFECTED; continue the WAVE normally. (3) Make NO commit-flow/skill change yet. Both decisions — af085575c disposition + whether to harden the /commit skill now — escalated to the operator; I will relay rulings on this channel. This blocks main pushes only, not WP execution; keep surfacing lane work.

Ruling on prg06ic-014 (recurring identity-reseed integration flake; corrected mechanism ACCEPTED — intra-CI maxWorkers=2 on one local postgres, reseed asserts GLOBAL counts → cross-worker contamination → membership FK violation at setup; NOT shared-stg-DB, NOT PR-caused). TRACK + FIX — do not settle for flaky-retry. Reasoning beyond the per-PR tax: this directly undermines the Step-11 51-suite flag-on integration sweep that ic-orch-011 made the WP-FLAG / WI-586 completeness gate. A KNOWN-flaky reseed suite is the real hazard — a genuine reseed regression gets waved off as 'just the flake, re-run', masking a real cutover bug; a flaky gate cannot be trusted to catch regressions. HOME: instantiate a WS-18 Item (title it test-infra if you prefer). It is test-infra-class and fleet-beneficial (de-flakes integration CI for ALL lanes incl PRG-10/11), BUT it gates the trustworthiness of the cutover's OWN close gate, so it belongs on the cutover timeline — not parked for a future CI initiative that may not land before WI-586. Mark it a SOFT precondition on the Step-11 sweep / WI-586 close. FIX APPROACH (your/executor call): lean serial — pin the reseed suite to --runInBand / a single-worker jest project, since it fundamentally asserts GLOBAL counts and cannot correctly co-run with other identity-touching suites; per-worker DB/schema is heavier infra for the same result; advisory-lock truncate+rebuild is the middle option. Cheapest robust = serialize the reseed suite. Priority P2 (systemic + gate-reliability; not blocking current WPs); keep flaky-retry as the band-aid until the fix lands. No operator decision; WAVE continues.

Operator-directed: ADMIT WI-782 into WS-18 as a PARKED item, and TAILOR it to fit the lane. WI-782 (page 3808bce9-1f7c-8126-b9e0-cc3c511b530b) = 'rework S4/S5 visibility-contract → read-time / derive-on-read per MMT-ADR-0022'; currently Captured, no Workstream, no Project. Actions: set Workstream=Identity Cutover (3808bce9-1f7c-81a2-9ea1-ee924aeaa0a8), Project=MentoMate (3658bce9-1f7c-8128-9f9b-fa7fcf75a13b), State=Parked, Stage=Backlog. CRITICAL FRAMING — keep the lane clean: this is V2-shell / visibility-contract FEATURE work, NOT cutover code. It is parked for VISIBILITY only and is explicitly NOT a WS-18 graduation gate — WS-18 graduates on its cutover scope (the WP set + WI-586); do NOT hold the lane close on WI-782; at graduation it migrates to the V2-shell / S4-S6 Initiative (to be stood up) or closes there. Do NOT dispatch an executor for it (deferred). SEQUENCING: coupled to WI-766/767 (ADR-0022 code convergence, ADR-cleanup stream) and informs the post-cutover S4/S5 BUILD — genuinely not actionable this wave. TAILORING: give it a trailing Workstream Order that sorts it AFTER all cutover WPs + WI-586 (e.g. 9999); preserve its derive-on-read corrective in AC/Description (no stored visibility column, no materialized moment kinds) per ADR-0022; scope/relay doc = _wip/adr-governance-correction/s4-s5-handover.md. Confirm admission on the channel.

Acks + rulings on prg06ic-015 + prg06ic-016. (015) WI-784/785 good; WI-784 standalone fine (assess archive-cleanup.ts at refine, do not drop). WI-780 branch-2 VERIFIED CLEAN by me — 5caa80923 = the decision-record doc only, zero ADR/canon files, so the ic-orch-004 in-lane fence held; the ADR-0020 section-2B over-claim correction is MY/operator cross-stream routing, NOT yours — you are clear on WI-780 pending review-close. Freeze-ack received. (016) WI-782 admission confirmed, exactly right (Order 9999, derive-on-read AC intact, parked/not-a-gate). WP-5 findings: (b) family-access parent-on-behalf reading legacy family_links — CONCUR, instantiate a new WS-18 v2-twin Item (P1; AC mirrors WP-2 v2-twin + ownership-scoping; canon-conformant; marked WP-FLAG precondition) — same treatment as WI-784. The Step-11 flag-on sweep (ic-orch-011) is the BACKSTOP not a substitute: BUILD the twin (a post-flag read of a dropped table cannot be left to a sweep to merely detect). (a) guardian-write suppression for credentialed has_own_account charges — RECLASSIFY: NOT a WP-FLAG/cutover precondition. You noted legacy behaves identically, so behavior is unchanged across the flip -> pre-existing policy-authority gap, not a flip-hazard; the ic-orch-005 in-flight-op audit is the wrong bucket. Instantiate (a) as a STANDALONE item (no workstream; Backlog; P2; tag policy-engine), NOT in WS-18 scope — do NOT gate the cutover on it and do NOT redesign the authority model from this lane (identity/policy canon territory); I will register it in the spillover register and route to the policy owner. NB WI-780 just went Reviewing->Awaiting Info: that is review-loop (your domain). If the reviewer is questioning whether branch-2 (decision record + routed ADR correction, no code) is a valid resolution: YES it is (ic-orch-004 sanctioned branch-2) — reflect that in the WI-780 AC so the agnostic reviewer can close it. WAVE continues.

prg06ic-017 discharge acknowledged — clean. (1) WI-780 determination SOUND: a memo-only PR cannot change RLS coverage, so the red database-rls-coverage is ambient/env-drift not a WI-780 effect; a doc-only deliverable has nothing to rework; the branch-2 DoD clarification is correct → reviewer closes Done. (2) WI-786 (family-access twin) + (3) WI-787 (policy gap, standalone) both correctly instantiated; I am registering WI-787 in the spillover register + routing to the identity/policy owner — you are clear of it. ON database-rls-coverage RED — do NOT let it dissolve as 'just flake': RLS is the security-enforcement floor, so DETERMINE it, don't assume. When WP-5 clears (not urgent): are the expected v2 RLS policies actually PRESENT in staging/prod and only MISSING in the local integration DB? Present in real envs → test-env drift (sibling to WI-785; relates to WI-684/688) → track as test-infra. Missing ANYWHERE real → REAL security gap (v2 tables without RLS), escalate immediately (P1, gates cutover). EITHER way it undermines the Step-11 flag-on 51-suite sweep reliability (a red RLS-coverage test in the sweep = close-gate untrustworthy), so it must be green before WP-FLAG/WI-586 close — fold into WI-785 reliability or a sibling. Check the ANY(array) query error at the same time (separate concrete bug). Track it (not a passing NB). Reseed-flake recurrence on WP-5 noted → confirms WI-785. WAVE continues.

prg06ic-018 — APPROVE FIX A NOW: dispatch the WP-9 conditional-legacy-parent seed-workaround immediately to unblock main (reversible, test-seed-only, self-inerting once B drops the legacy tables; a RED main blocks the WHOLE fleet — PRG-10/11 too). VERIFICATION GATE: A must yield a FULLY GREEN test-seed run, not just the two named FKs — sweep for OTHER legacy-FK children hiding behind the first failure (any v2-seed insert whose FK targets a legacy parent). Four corrections (this incident reaches past main): (1) REVERSE ic-orch-010 / RESTORE ic-orch-009 — rehome-before-drop IS a real precondition for COMMITTED MIGRATIONS. My acceptance of your no-CASCADE-drop proof was true ON STAGING ONLY and over-generalized: staging cutover (m-repoint + M-DROP) was MANUAL, NOT committed migrations, so CI/PROD on fresh migrations still carry legacy profiles/subscriptions + their subjects/quota_pools FKs. (2) B IS THE CUTOVER CORE, NOT close-polish — commit m-repoint + M-DROP as REAL migrations, tested against a FRESH committed-migration DB; anchor it as load-bearing WI-586 scope NOW so A does not relieve pressure and let B drift. Prod can ONLY be cut via committed migrations; B is the deliverable. (3) ic-orch-011 COMPLETENESS BACKSTOP INVALIDATED as written: a green flag-on STAGING sweep does NOT prove completeness — staging passes BECAUSE it was manually repointed, so it is structurally BLIND to committed-migration FK/schema defects (this exact class). The Step-11 close gate must run the FULL suite against a DB built ONLY from committed migrations (what CI just did → red), NOT staging. VERIFY what your Step-11 sweep targets; if staging, correct to a fresh-migration DB. (4) STAGING-PARITY (for B, not blocking A): is staging a faithful cutover or a hand-built approximation that diverges from committed-migration output? If hand-built, WI-586 must REBUILD staging from committed migrations to confirm parity BEFORE prod — do not trust hand-cutover staging as the prod rehearsal. No blame — I co-signed the adjudication; the correction is what matters. Holding-main-until-green is correct. Report A green + your Step-11-target finding.

prg06ic-019 — full discharge ENDORSED, all 6 points correct (ic-010 reversed/ic-009 restored; A dispatched WI-788 with the sweep-ALL-legacy-FK-children + fresh-committed-migration-DB + red-green-revert gate; B = WI-586 core; Step-11 retargeted to a flag-ON committed-migration run; staging-parity rebuild folded into WI-586). Clean work. ONE ADDITION on your (5) finding — it is bigger than a close-gate fix: CI runs integration flag-OFF, so the v2 code paths have had ZERO committed-migration integration coverage, which means ALL the already-merged v2 WPs (WP-2..WP-9) landed under flag-OFF CI — WP-9's defect class is very likely NOT unique. So do not ONLY wire the flag-ON committed-migration integration run as the WI-586 close gate (keep that, permanent): ALSO run it EARLY as a one-off DIAGNOSTIC the moment fix A greens main (local or a throwaway CI job; IDENTITY_V2_ENABLED=true; fresh committed-migration DB), to ENUMERATE any other committed-migration FK/schema defects across the merged WPs NOW — turning 'discover them one painful main-red at a time' into 'find them all at once, early', with lead time to fix before WP-FLAG/WI-586. Report what the early flag-ON diagnostic surfaces. Standing by for A green.

prg06ic-020 received — main UNBLOCKED, fleet freed. Verification gate SATISFIED and then some: the full transitive sweep (write the 3 legacy parents accounts/profiles/subscriptions → all ~55 FK children resolve, no per-child code) is the right shape, and validating 53 suites/409 tests 0-fail against a FRESH committed-migration DB (drizzle-kit migrate) is exactly the corrected target (committed migrations, not staging) — ic-orch-017 loop closed. Early flag-ON diagnostic dispatch + folding the 2 Codex P2s (defaultAppContext drop; seedAccountDeletionScheduled missing legacy accounts parent — latent, same FK class) into it = correct; both must be resolved before the WP-FLAG/WI-586 close gate, and the diagnostic scopes them. The diagnostic enumeration is the real signal now — it tells us how much committed-migration debt the merged WP-2..9 carry. Standing by for those findings. Clean, fast unblock — well done.

prg06ic-021 diagnostic findings received — this is the signal, and it confirms the hypothesis (WP-9's class is NOT unique: ~5 real flag-ON committed-migration defects across merged WP-2..9 + the structural root). APPROVE your proposed filing — FILE THEM into WS-18 as NEW follow-up items (do NOT reopen the closed parent WPs); all are WP-FLAG preconditions. Shaping + invariants: (i) STRUCTURAL ROOT (buildIntegrationEnv never sets IDENTITY_V2_ENABLED → zero flag-ON HTTP-route integration coverage) = own WI, HIGHEST leverage (the enabler + the permanent WI-586 close-gate mechanism). SEQUENCING INVARIANT: land the flag-ON committed-migration coverage NON-BLOCKING first (allowed-red / separate job); make it the REQUIRED close gate ONLY after D1/D2/D3/D4/FG1 + :709 burn down — flipping it on while reds exist would red main (the i18n-ratchet pattern). So (i) enables+enforces, does NOT block on the others; the others burn down against it. (ii) D2+D3 → WP-8 Inngest seeder-coverage bundle. (iii) D1 → billing seeder/v2 (WP-7-adjacent). (iv) FG1 → consent restoreConsent v2 twin — lean P1 (consent=COPPA/GDPR AND it is a FALSE-GREEN, the most dangerous class). (v) D4 → standalone P1 query-bug. CRITICAL NOTE: D4 answers ONLY the ANY(array) query-bug half of the ic-orch-016 RLS-red; the RLS-POLICY-PRESENCE half (real security gap vs test-env drift — are the v2 RLS policies actually applied in staging/prod?) is STILL OPEN → do the staging pg_policies check; do NOT let D4's fix close out the security question. (vi) :709 → fold into WP-FLAG seeder work (good catch that :612 is clean, not a defect). Priorities your call at refine; lean P1 on FG1/D4/D1, P2 on D2/D3. COMPLETENESS CAVEAT: flag-ON INTEGRATION coverage ≠ full coverage — :709-class surfaces (Maestro-only / no CI test) stay blind even after (i); the layered pre-WP-FLAG completeness story = this integration burndown + the staging-parity rebuild (B) + a flag-ON E2E/Maestro pass. Make this burndown an explicit pre-WP-FLAG wave in your tracker. File them; standing by for the 2nd diagnostic's reconciliation.

Filing CONFIRMED — WI-789..793 + :709 match the approved shaping exactly; ratchet invariant in WI-789 AC, RLS-policy-presence security half explicitly preserved on WI-791 AC + as Task #13, burndown wave + layered completeness in the tracker. Proceed — no changes. The one thread I am watching for ESCALATION: Task #13 (staging pg_policies check) — if the v2 RLS policies turn out actually MISSING in a real env (not just local drift), that is a real security gap → escalate immediately (P1, gates the cutover), do not let it ride as test-infra. Everything else burns down normally. Standing by for the 2nd-diagnostic reconciliation.

ESCALATION received — the ic-orch-021 watch fired correctly; real-security-gap branch. RULINGS: (1) AUTHORIZED — dispatch the READ-ONLY prod pg_policies/relrowsecurity check IMMEDIATELY (doppler -c prd, read-only, NO writes; reads system catalogs only, not customer data — allowed; AGENTS.md bans db:push WRITES, not read-only queries). This is the urgent unknown; authorizing autonomously (reversible). Report prod status ASAP. (2) FILE the P1 RLS-remediation WI now (tracked) with a PRELIMINARY scope (re-apply 0027+0085+policy DDL idempotently + apply the absent 0113, OR a targeted remediation migration — your forensic call) — BUT EXECUTION IS GATED: re-applying RLS to PROD is a prod-schema WRITE = genuinely hard-to-reverse → OPERATOR-OWNED; do NOT execute against prod without operator sign-off + the prod-status finding in hand. Staging (zero-customer) may be scoped/dry-run but coordinate before applying. File it in WS-18 for visibility but flag operator-ownership of execution (DB-integrity + security spanning staging+prod = above-lane). (3) FILE the investigate-prohibited-db:push + add-a-guard item (read-only forensic fine to run now; the guard — block db:push against stg/prod — is additive safety; pairs with the shared-infra-safety thread incl. the /commit-fork hazard). CONTEXT this confirms: staging is not merely divergent — it is SECURITY-BROKEN (prohibited db:push recreated tables from the RLS-less TS schema). This HARDENS fix B: prod must be cut via committed migrations (which CREATE the RLS), and the WI-586 staging-parity REBUILD must replace hand-cut staging with a committed-migration-built one. NUANCE correct + noted: no live leak today (neondb_owner bypasses RLS; isolation app-level) — the gap activates at the app_user switch, which MUST NOT proceed until staging+PROD RLS is repaired (now a hard CUT-B/app_user-switch precondition). I am HOLDING the operator notification pending your prod-status finding: prod also stripped → I escalate to operator immediately; prod clean → staging-rebuild + investigation (forward work). DECISION: (A) The reviewer MUST validate durable-fix-on-current-main against ORIGIN/MAIN (the canonical GitHub squash-merged state), NEVER the local working-checkout HEAD.
